title: potentilla chrysantha subsp. amphibola
intro: Potentilla chrysantha subsp. amphibola, also known as Korean cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla chrysantha subsp. amphibola include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Potentilla chrysantha subsp. amphibola can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
